evals.prompt: I received code review feedback on my PR that adds a caching layer to our API. The reviewer left these comments: (1) 'This cache invalidation logic has a race condition when two concurrent writes happen.' (2) 'Consider using Redis TTL instead of manual expiration tracking.' (3) 'The variable name `d` should be more descriptive.' (4) 'Why didn't you add integration tests for the cache miss path?' (5) 'Nice use of the decorator pattern here.' Help me categorize this feedback and plan my responses.
assertions: categorizes the race condition comment as blocking/critical since it affects correctness
categorizes the variable naming comment as non-blocking/style since it does not affect behavior
recommends acknowledging the positive feedback about the decorator pattern
suggests writing integration tests for the cache miss path as the reviewer requested
provides a concrete response plan for addressing the race condition with specific technical approach

evals.prompt: Two reviewers on my PR disagree. Alice says I should use an interface-based approach for the payment processor abstraction because it enables easier testing. Bob says I should use a concrete class with dependency injection because the interface adds unnecessary indirection for a single implementation. Both are senior engineers. How should I handle this?
assertions: recommends surfacing the conflict explicitly rather than silently choosing one side
analyzes the trade-offs of both approaches with specific technical reasoning
suggests a resolution mechanism such as a brief discussion thread or synchronous conversation
considers the YAGNI principle regarding the single implementation concern
recommends documenting the decision rationale regardless of which approach is chosen
